hypotheses.claim: The manuscript's text structure reflects a mix of prose and verse, with the herbal section being primarily composed of short, rhyming couplets.
evidence_for: Herbal section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
evidence_against: No clear evidence of rhyme or meter in other sections
hypotheses.test: Compute the average word length and frequency of consecutive words with identical first/last glyphs
tests_remaining: Compute the average word length and frequency of consecutive words with identical first/last glyphs
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would suggest a more complex text structure than previously thought

hypotheses.claim: The manuscript uses a combination of substitution and transposition ciphers in the biological section.
evidence_for: Biological section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
evidence_against: No clear evidence of transposition patterns
hypotheses.test: Compute the frequency of consecutive words with identical glyphs in different positions
hypotheses.test_metric: Frequency of consecutive word pairs with identical glyphs
tests_remaining: Compute the frequency of consecutive words with identical glyphs in different positions
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would explain the higher entropy levels in the biological section

hypotheses.claim: The text uses a simple substitution cipher on an unknown language.
evidence_for: Glyph entropy is lower than expected for Latin or Italian
evidence_against: No clear evidence of an unknown language
hypotheses.test: Compute the frequency of word-final glyphs and compare to known languages
tests_remaining: Compute the frequency of word-final glyphs and compare to known languages
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would suggest a new language or dialect

hypotheses.claim: The Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dialects.
evidence_for: Currier A/B split has significant differences in vocabulary and entropy levels
evidence_against: No clear evidence of language shift or dialectical variation
hypotheses.test: Compute the similarity between word lists for Currier A and B
tests_remaining: Compute the similarity between word lists for Currier A and B
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would suggest a genuine linguistic difference
